steps.instruction: Look at this word: jumped. What is the base word?
steps.explanation: The base word is 'jump'. The ending '-ed' has been added to it.

steps.instruction: The ending '-ed' tells us the action already happened (past tense).
steps.work: jump = happening now, jumped = already happened
steps.explanation: Adding '-ed' changes the word to past tense. 'I jump' means right now. 'I jumped' means it already happened.

steps.instruction: Add '-ed' to the word 'walk'. What does it mean?
steps.explanation: The '-ed' ending works the same way: 'walked' means the walking already happened. This ending is one of the most common in English.

steps.instruction: Look at this word: running. What is the base word?
steps.explanation: The base word is 'run'. The ending '-ing' has been added. Notice the 'n' was doubled before adding '-ing'.

steps.instruction: The ending '-ing' tells us the action is happening right now.
steps.work: run = to run, running = happening right now
steps.explanation: Adding '-ing' shows the action is in progress. 'The dog is running' means the dog is doing it right now.

steps.work: played (-ed) = already happened, playing (-ing) = happening now
steps.explanation: The endings change when the action happens. '-ed' means it is done. '-ing' means it is still going on.
